titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Thacher Hurd Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:19

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (41 boxes) + 1 art, 1 broadside
did.abstract: The collection consists of original artwork, writings, dummies, proofs, photographs, notebooks and sketchbooks, publishing correspondence and contracts, printed materials, memorabilia, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the career of children's book author Thacher Hurd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of original artwork, writings, dummies, proofs, photographs, notebooks and sketchbooks, publishing correspondence and contracts, printed materials, memorabilia, and computer and audiovisual media documenting the career of children's book author Thacher Hurd.

Included are illustrations and manuscripts for Mama Don't Allow , Art Dog , Zoom City , and Moo Cow Kaboom! , as well as unpublished works by Hurd and books he illustrated for other authors. The collection also contains photographs and artwork for projects unrelated to his children's books, files related to Peaceable Kingdom Press, and a small amount of personal papers, which include early drawings and paintings by Hurd and artwork he created in college. Among the correspondents are Clement Hurd, Edith Thacher Hurd, Olivia Scott Hurd, James Marshall, Clyde Robert Bulla, Helen Frost, and Susan Terris, in addition to his literary agency Curtis Brown Ltd., and editors and executives from Harper & Row, Crown Publishers, and Henry Holt and Co.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 40 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 41 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Thatcher Hurd Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Windle Antiquarian Bookseller on the Jockey Hollow Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Thacher Hurd Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Thacher Hurd (1949-)
bioghist.p: Thacher Hurd, son of children's book creators Clement Hurd and Edith Thacher Hurd, is the author and illustrator of more than 25 books for children, including Mystery on the Docks (1983), Mama Don't Allow (1984), Pea Patch Jig (1986), Art Dog (1996), Zoom City (1998), Moo Cow Kaboom! (2003), and Bongo Fishing (2011). Hurd was born in 1949 in Burlington, Vermont and graduated from the California College of Arts and Crafts. He published his first book, The Old Chair , in 1978. His works have been adapted into spoken books, a musical, and an opera. Mama Don't Allow won the Boston Globe Horn Book Award and was named one of Time Magazine 's "100 Best Children's Books of all Time." In 1983 Thacher and his wife Olivia Scott Hurd founded Peaceable Kingdom Press, a publisher of cards and posters adapted from children's books. Thacher and Olivia Hurd live in Berkeley, California, and have two children, Manton and Nicholas.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Children's Books, 1950-2017. II. Photographs, Artwork, and Printed Materials, 1972-2017, undated. III. Correspondence, 1968-2016, undated. IV. Journals and Notebooks, 1976-2014, undated. V. Peaceable Kingdom Press, 1983-2002. VI. Personal Papers, 1949-2003. VII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1993-2004, undated.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Folder titles in brackets were supplied by staff during processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

did.physdesc: (22 boxes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily documents the publication of children's books written and illustrated by Thacher Hurd, including Mama Don't Allow , Pea Patch Jig , Blackberry Ramble , Art Dog , Zoom City , and Bad Frogs , as well as Watercolor for the Artistically Undiscovered , which was reissued in 2017 as Paint this Book . Also included are manuscripts, artwork, and dummies for unpublished works; files for books Hurd illustrated for other authors; and materials related to promoting Hurd's works, such as publicity photos and animal hats worn by Hurd during school visits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Written and Illustrated by Thacher Hurd, Writings of Others Illustrated by Thacher Hurd, Manuscripts, Studies, and Mockups for Unpublished Stories, and Manuscripts and Ephemera Relating to Children's Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains preliminary drawings, gouache, pastel, and watercolor illustrations, notes, manuscript drafts, dummy books and mockups, proofs, and memorabilia for 22 children's books written and illustrated by Thacher Hurd, including his first published work, The Old Chair . Also included are correspondence, legal documents, financial records, reviews, publicity materials, and items used in Hurd's research, such as magazine and newspaper clippings and photographs.

did.unittitle: Study, "A dog asleep on a rug," watercolor
Study, "Father and Mother are sitting by the fire," gouache painting
Original gouache and watercolor illustrations and illustrations on transparencies
Early color dummies, typescripts, copies of the "Hobo Times," sketches of trains, jacket dummy, correspondence, single painted transparency
Early color dummies, sketches of trains, proofs
Original gouache illustrations with tissue overlays with printed text affixed
Axle The Freeway Cat (Harper & Row, 1981), color studies
Correspondence with Scholastic, Curtis Brown, and Viking Brown, typescripts, "tramps in literature" index, copyright registration certificate
Dummies with original illustrations and pasted title text, dustjacket proof
Color proof of entire book, uncut and unfolded
Color transparency of a double page spread
Hobo Dog in the Ghost Town (Scholastic, 1985)
Dummies, original gouache illustrations, copyright documentation, printed proof of jacket design, draft, typed editorial comments, correspondence with Curtis Brown Ltd.
Original gouache illustrations including the cover design, and drafts, holograph and typescript
Mockup with original watercolor illustrations and pasted typed text with handwritten additions, labeled 1975, "text revised 1985"
Original watercolor studies (peas dancing, farmer hoeing)
Original jacket design in gouache, painted transparency for title, original gouache illustrations, some with tissue overlays with printed text affixed
Hand-calligraphed music manuscript by Dan Emmett
Original watercolor illustration, mice approaching the barn at night, a farmer pushing cow into the barn
Original sketches and illustrations in pencil and gouache including pencil sketch of title page
Transparencies, photocopies of layouts, pencil sketches
Correspondence with Crown Publishers Inc., jacket dimension chart, typed manuscripts
A Night in the Swamp (Carousel, 1987), printed and bound mockup with pasted text
Little Mouse's Big Valentine (HarperCollins, 1990), original gouache illustrations, jacket art, page dummies with original illustrations and pasted-on text

Dummy with pencil sketches and pasted in printed text, typed manuscript with Crown Publishers copyright page, correspondence with Crown, and assorted pencil sketches, original studies in gouache, clippings of cats, and photocopies
Photocopied dummy, original studies in gouache, clippings of cats, and photocopies
Original gouache illustrations with tissue overlays with printed text affixed
Original art for jacket design, two snowy scenes
Watercolor for the Artistically Undiscovered with John Cassidy (1992), reissued as Paint this Book (2017)
Ultra Dog (precursor to Art Dog ), typed and handwritten manuscript, notes, dummy with original artwork, magazine clipping and a copy of Canine Quarterly
Ultra Dog , original studies, drawings torn out of sketchbook
Layout, color proofs, sketch of jacket flap
Dustjackets and color proof of the book pages
Reviews, photographs of visits to bookstores, magazines, clippings, etc. [includes correspondence, artwork, and legal papers]
Dummy made of color prints, original pencil sketches and gouache illustrations, photographs, and magazine clippings
Dummy made of color prints, original pencil sketches and gouache illustrations, photographs, and magazine clippings
Original gouache illustration of cats playing instruments [print]
Gouache, charcoal, and pencil studies, illustrations and printed proofs
Typed and handwritten manuscripts, several versions of the text, printed dustjacket, various studies, clippings and notes
Dummies from various stages of the design development
Transparencies, photos, etc. [includes slides and one dummy]
Original watercolor illustrations and pencil sketches
Pkg #1: misc. docs including edited manuscripts, handwritten notes, and sticky notes
Pkg #1: misc. docs including edited manuscripts, handwritten notes, and sticky notes
Pkg #2: misc. manuscript chapters, pictures of cars
Writings of Others Illustrated by Hurd

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials related to two children's books written by other authors and illustrated by Thacher Hurd, including original gouache illustrations, a sketchbook, and a mockup.

did.unittitle: Wheel Away by Dayle Ann Dodds (HarperCollins, 1989), original illustration, collage and painted paper
Manuscripts, Studies, Mockups for Unpublished Stories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts of story ideas, manuscripts, illustrations, dummies, and mockups for yet-unpublished works by Thacher Hurd, including "It Came from the Subway" and "Jason's House."

did.unittitle: Board book ideas, mockups and typescripts of four unpublished stories
Come and Have Fun by Edith Thacher Hurd, idea for re-illustrating
Dummy for an unpublished book without a title about a little yellow house
"Eagle Eyed Eddie," an unpublished story (based upon a story written by a friend as kid), manuscript and mockup
"It Came From the Subway," mockups, manuscript, and various research materials
"It Came From the Subway," mockups, manuscript, and various research materials
Dummy with original pen and ink illustrations and typed text attached by paper clips
Manuscripts and five mockups for an unpublished book that has had several names including "Egypt Station" and "Harry & Albert"
"Mattie and the Chicken Thief," "Swamp Thing," illustrations
My Uncle Nikos by Julie Delton, dummy with original artwork (eventually published with art from another illustrator)
Notebook and nine manuscripts with story ideas and a watercolor sketch for a story idea about a dog detective
Story ideas - handwritten fragments and notebooks
Typed and handwritten manuscripts and mock-ups for around 20 unpublished stories
Typed and handwritten manuscripts for around 15 unpublished books and a little correspondence
Manuscripts and Ephemera relating to Children's Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains publicity materials, artwork, correspondence, notes for speeches, photographs, and reviews of books by Thacher Hurd, as well as letters from children, three animal hats Hurd wore during visits to schools, and a dog puppet head possibly used for stop-motion animation. Also includes copies of a printed libretto of Mystery on the Docks , a book by Hurd that was adapted as an opera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder or object title.

did.unittitle: Block print, mice in the snow
Business card dummy (mouse in bait shop from Mystery on the Docks with Thacher's name and address pasted on)
Dog drawings, two watercolor sketches of dogs
The Labyrinth of Story: Narrative as Creative Construction. A Participatory Study by Beverly Vaughn Hock, 1999. Bound dissertation, University of San Francisco.
Material relating to memorials for William C. Morris and Marilyn Marlow including typewritten manuscripts, memorial programs, and a photograph
Miscellaneous ephemera: letters and reviews written by children, notes for speeches, magazines, newspapers and clipped articles, photographs and negatives, correspondence
Miscellaneous prints, cards, illustrations, announcements, watercolors, etching, CD cover, etc.
Mystery on the Docks, An Opera in 3 Short Acts by Edward Barnes, printed libretto
Notebook containing handwritten poem and story fragments
Original artwork for a reading poster (crocodile in a suit reading a book), black and white watercolor
Publicity photos and negatives, mainly TH with some including CH, PTH, and Olivia
Reviews, articles, newspaper clippings, and other ephemera
Something About the Author , volume 123 [entry about Hurd]
Speeches and autobiographical fragments, magazine articles and newspaper clippings
Studies for Berkeley Library poster, three watercolor sketches of animals reading
Three animal hats for school visits: cow, bear, and crocodile

did.physdesc: (9 boxes) + 1 art, 1 broadside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, photogravures, negatives, and original artwork unrelated to children's books created by Thacher Hurd.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Photographs, Artwork, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains black-and-white and color photographic prints, photogravures, etchings, negatives, and contact sheets, as well as two copper plates for etchings. Hurd often depicts locations he visited with his family such as Maine, Vermont, California, Hawaii, and India. The series also contains "street" scenes of people in cities and images of a peace march in New York.

did.unittitle: Alt. photo negs. Spring 2002 Mary Shisler class at Kala + 8x10 sleeves
Black-and-white photogravures of Maine and other locations
Black-and-white photographs, photogravures, and etchings of Maine
Etched copper plate for a photogravure
Etched copper plate, image of a barn
Maine Doubles Summer '02 [black and white negatives]
Negatives and contact sheets of photos taken between 2000 and 2012 [removed from binders]

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order, with undated negatives filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous CA, New York City, Jasper, Berk. Library
Flowers; Great Cranberry ME; Bristol Pond
Tilden - Briones; Tahoe; Flowers; San Pablo Reservoir; Hot Rods
California; Point Reyes; Mt. Tam; Healdsburg; Walker Creek
Non-fogged glossy [image of a man with sign reading "Hungry and homeless please help"]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains watercolor, pencil, ink, pastel, chalk, and gouache illustrations of animals, reptiles, and dinosaurs, which may have been created by Thacher Hurd for school or library posters. Additionally included are drawings for a book about Sigmund Freud and aliens, works inspired by altarpieces, and still lifes.

did.unittitle: Drawings for "Home for the Holidays" poster
Drawings of "After School Monster," mice, cats, dogs, frogs, alligators, fish
Drawings of animals, Christmas cards, other personal cards
Drawing of laid table by window, blue and orange gouache with chalk pastel
Drawings of mice, bears, and collage
Gouache painting of plants by window
Miscellaneous sketches relating to book about Sigmund Freud and aliens
Original art for a birthday card
Pastel drawing of people sleeping in clouds
Sketchbook with pencil sketches, manuscript text, and watercolor studies
Watercolors, ink and pencil drawings, including cover art for an alphabet book
Watercolors, ink, pencil, and gouache drawings
Watercolors, ink, pencil, and gouache drawings, including "Creature Watching TV," "Home for the Holidays" artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes of posters, postcards, and holiday cards designed by Thacher Hurd for the Berkeley Public Library and other organizations, as well as posters advertising works by Hurd and other authors.

did.physdesc: (1 box)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains publishing correspondence and legal papers pertaining to books by Thacher Hurd, and personal and family correspondence of Hurd, his wife Olivia Scott, parents Edith Thacher Hurd and Clement Hurd, and children Manton and Nicholas. Among the legal papers are an agreement with Reading Rainbow to air a performance of Mystery on the Docks , an opera based on a book by Hurd. Correspondents include his agency Curtis Brown Ltd., editors and executives from Harper & Row, Crown Publishers, and Henry Holt and Co., illustrator James Marshall, and authors Clyde Robert Bulla, Helen Frost, and Susan Terris. The series additionally contains copies of letters from Hurd to Maurice Sendak, and a small amount of artwork, writings, and printed materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Correspondence can also be found in Series I. Children's Books.

did.unittitle: "A Private Letter to J.T.H. from C.G.H., March 6, 1970, Mill Valley"
Letters to Olivia and Thacher Hurd
Various publishing emails: Margaret Ferguson at Farrar Straus, Amy Ehrlich at Candlewick

did.physdesc: (1 box)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks and journals contain Thacher Hurd's personal writings, travel diary entries, story ideas and drafts, poems, and illustrations, as well as technical photographic notes, and notes on timed and free writing practices.

did.unittitle: Travel diary from 1989, a journey taken all over Europe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of the Hurd family and an unidentified woman are taped to the endpapers.

did.unittitle: Journals containing timed and free writing practices

did.physdesc: (1 box)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence with children's book authors and illustrators, including Ian Falconer, Kevin Henkes, Margret E. Rey, and James Marshall, financial records, photographs, and posters, catalogs, and other printed materials related to Peaceable Kingdom Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a poster for The Runaway Bunny signed by Clement Hurd.

did.unittitle: Folder of correspondence, original sketches, and other ephemera relating to the business

did.physdesc: (3 boxes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains artwork and writings by Thacher Hurd from the age of four until his college years, artwork by his son Nicholas Hurd and others, biographical information on Hurd and his parents, and obituaries and condolence letters related to his mother's death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Early and Art School Works, and Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes paintings, a pencil and ink drawing, and an illustrated manuscript created by Hurd when he was a child, and figure studies and gouache illustrations from his time as a student at the California College of Arts and Crafts.

did.unittitle: Pencil and ink drawing of Santa Claus
Mr. Charlie's Camping Trip (original illustrated manuscript of Thacher's first book)
Still Life (Fruit Bowl) (Thacher's first framed work) [4th grade]
Figure studies from life, mainly in sepia chalk with some in color pastels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains biographical materials about Thacher Hurd, book reviews, and articles featuring Olivia Hurd and Peaceable Kingdom Press; obituaries for Edith Thacher Hurd and condolence letters to Thacher Hurd after her death from author Barbara Quick, activist Sally Lilienthal, and others; papers related to Manton and Nicholas Hurd; artwork; photographs; and a small amount of family correspondence. Also included are materials related to Hurd's early life, such as programs for an eighth grade play and his graduation from Marin Country Day School in 1963.

did.unittitle: Artwork and photographs by other artists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped on the verso: "EXPOSITION UNIVERSELLE DE 1855 / MENTION HONORABLE / PAPIER, CARTON, CHASSIS, TOILES / ANTI-PONCE pour le PASTEL / P.L. Breveté PARIS / MARQUE DÉPOSÉE." Also includes a handwritten inscription: "Mr. Edmund Dwight 191 Malborough St. Boston."

did.physdesc: (3 boxes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of audio and video recordings of interviews with Thacher Hurd and performances of his works, and digital files for photographs and children's books written and illustrated by Hurd, including Bongo Fishing and Moo-Cow Kaboom! .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 41 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.p: Arranged in chronological order.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
